record.field: SuiteCRM is a full-featured open-source CRM for sales, marketing and customer service, giving businesses a self-hosted alternative to proprietary suites.

SuiteCRM is a full-featured open-source CRM for sales, marketing and customer service, giving businesses a self-hosted alternative to proprietary suites.

Built in PHP as a community fork of SugarCRM, SuiteCRM covers the whole commercial cycle: accounts, contacts, leads and opportunities, quotes and contracts, marketing campaigns and case management. It ships with workflow automation and a Studio for custom modules and fields, so sales, marketing and support teams can model their own processes and keep sensitive commercial data on infrastructure they control.

Key features
  • 360° customer view: accounts, contacts, leads and opportunities
  • Sales pipeline with quotes, contracts, products and forecasting
  • Marketing campaigns, target lists and reusable email templates
  • Case management, activities calendar and a customer self-service portal
  • Workflow automation plus custom modules and fields through Studio
  • REST/GraphQL API, role-based security and reporting dashboards
Best for

Small and mid-sized businesses and teams that want a mature, self-hosted CRM as an alternative to Salesforce or SugarCRM, keeping their sales and customer data on their own infrastructure.

services: suitecrm: image: bitnami/suitecrm:latest restart: unless-stopped ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8080' environment: - SUITECRM_DATABASE_HOST=db - SUITECRM_DATABASE_PORT_NUMBER=3306 - SUITECRM_DATABASE_NAME={{ db_name }} - SUITECRM_DATABASE_USER={{ db_user }} - SUITECRM_DATABASE_PASSWORD={{ db_password }} - SUITECRM_USERNAME=admin - SUITECRM_PASSWORD={{ admin_password }} - SUITECRM_EMAIL={{ admin_email }} - SUITECRM_HOST={{ domain }} - SUITECRM_VALIDATE_USER_IP=no - ALLOW_EMPTY_PASSWORD=no volumes: - ./suitecrm_data:/bitnami/suitecrm depends_on: db: condition: service_healthy container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network db: image: mariadb:10.11 restart: unless-stopped environment: - MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD={{ db_root_password }} - MYSQL_DATABASE={{ db_name }} - MYSQL_USER={{ db_user }} - MYSQL_PASSWORD={{ db_password }} volumes: - ./db_data:/var/lib/mysql healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - healthcheck.sh --connect --innodb_initialized interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 start_period: 30s container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump de la base de APLICACIÓN. Clave: usa MYSQL_PWD (env), NUNCA `-p` — con `-p` y # password vacío (apps con MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD) mysql espera un prompt por stdin # y CUELGA. Prefiere el usuario de app (MYSQL_USER/PASSWORD/DATABASE); cae a root solo si # no hay usuario de app (excluye schemas de sistema, que romperían la auth al restaurar). docker compose exec -T db sh -c ' # MariaDB 11+ renombró mysqldump→mariadb-dump y mysql→mariadb: detectamos el binario. DUMP=$(command -v mysqldump || command -v mariadb-dump); CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_DATABASE:-}" ]; then export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD" exec "$DUMP" -u"$MYSQL_USER" --single-transaction --databases "$MYSQL_DATABASE" fi export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}" DBS=$("$CLI" -uroot -N -e "SHOW DATABASES" | grep -Ev "^(mysql|sys|information_schema|performance_schema)$") [ -z "$DBS" ] && { echo "REVISAR: sin bases de aplicacion para respaldar" >&2; exit 0; } exec "$DUMP" -uroot --single-transaction --databases $DBS ' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "mysqldump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d db # Readiness + restore en UN exec (credenciales DENTRO del contenedor, con MYSQL_PWD → # jamás cuelga esperando un prompt de password). Prefiere el usuario de app; cae a root. docker compose exec -T db sh -c ' CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then U="$MYSQL_USER"; export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD"; else U=root; export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}"; fi for i in $(seq 1 60); do "$CLI" -u"$U" -e "SELECT 1" >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 2; done exec "$CLI" -u"$U" ' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "restore MySQL OK"
